---
title: "Chapter 5 Visits and how we can use them"
teaching: 0
exercises: 0
---

:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: questions 

- What are visits and how can they be used ?

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: objectives

- Know that visits are recorded in the visit_occurrence table
- Know each visit is unique to a person
- Understand that other tables link to visits
- Understand how visits can be used to find co-occurrence of other events

::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

## Introduction

TODO not sure if this wants to be in a separate episode or a more general one about linking tables. It could always be renamed later.

The `visit_occurrence` table contains [events where Persons engage with the healthcare system for a duration of time](https://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm54.html#visit_occurrence).


The main clinical tables `condition_occurrence`, `measurement`, `observation` and `drug_exposure` contain a `visit_occurrence_id` that links to this table.


`visit_concept_id` sepcifies the kind of visit that took place using standardised OMOP concepts. These include `Inpatient visit`, `Emergency Room Visit` and `Outpatient Visit`. Inpatient visits can last for longer than one day.    

The `visit_detail` table can contain information about time periods shorter than the visit (for example transfer between wards).

Here we will use some mock data from the OMOPSketch package to illustrate.

Load data.

```r
install.packages("OmopSketch")
library(OmopSketch)
library(dplyr)
cdm <- mockOmopSketch()
```

See what kinds of visits there are in the data, for this example it is only Inpatient.

```r
cdm$visit_occurrence |> 
  count(visit_concept_id) |> 
  left_join(cdm$concept, by = c("visit_concept_id" = "concept_id"))

```

Look for patients with more than one visit.

```r
visit_counts <- cdm$visit_occurrence |>
  group_by(person_id) |>
  summarise(n_visits = n()) |>
  filter(n_visits > 1) |>
  collect() |>   
  arrange(desc(n_visits))

visit_counts |> head()

```

Look at visits for a single patient.

```r
example_person_id <- visit_counts$person_id[1]

patient_visits <- cdm$visit_occurrence |>
  filter(person_id == example_person_id) |>
  left_join(cdm$concept, by = c("visit_concept_id" = "concept_id")) |>
  select(
    visit_occurrence_id, visit_start_date, visit_end_date, concept_name
  ) |>
  arrange(visit_start_date) |>
  collect()

patient_visits

```

```output
  visit_occurrence_id visit_start_date visit_end_date concept_name        
1              192596 1998-03-24       1998-03-25     Inpatient Visit
2              192623 1997-02-20       1997-02-21     Inpatient Visit
3              192594 1932-08-06       1932-08-07     Inpatient Visit
4              192595 1923-01-14       1923-01-15     Inpatient Visit
```

Here is an example where the visit can make a difference to an analysis.
Imagine we want to look at which drugs are associated with which conditions for patients.

If we were to join tables by `person_id` as we have seen in previous exercises we could get conditions and drugs that were separated by many years. Instead we can include `visit_occurrence_id` in the join to get co-occurrence of conditions and drugs by visit.
TODO in this example the co-occurrences are meaningless, need better example data.

```r

co_occurrence_by_visit <- cdm$condition_occurrence |>
  # Join with drug_exposure on person and visit
  inner_join(cdm$drug_exposure, by = c("person_id", "visit_occurrence_id")) |>
  select(
    person_id,
    visit_occurrence_id,
    condition_concept_id,
    drug_concept_id
  ) |>
  # Count how often each conditionâ€“drug pair co-occurs in a visit
  group_by(condition_concept_id, drug_concept_id) |>
  summarise(n_visits = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  
  # Join to get condition name
  left_join(
    cdm$concept |> select(concept_id, concept_name),
    by = c("condition_concept_id" = "concept_id")
  ) |>
  rename(condition_name = concept_name) |>
  
  # Join to get drug name
  left_join(
    cdm$concept |> select(concept_id, concept_name),
    by = c("drug_concept_id" = "concept_id")
  ) |>
  rename(drug_name = concept_name) |>
  collect() |> 
  
  # Arrange for easier viewing
  arrange(desc(n_visits))

```


::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: keypoints 



::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

[r-markdown]: https://rmarkdown.rstudio.com/
